# dependencytrack_license (Resource)

Manages a custom license in Dependency-Track. Dependency-Track has no endpoint to update a license, so any change forces the license to be replaced.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dependencytrack_license" "example" {
  license_id   = "Acme-1.0"
  name         = "Acme Corporation License 1.0"
  text         = "Permission is hereby granted..."
  comment      = "Internal license used by Acme projects"
  osi_approved = false
  fsf_libre    = false
  see_also = [
    "https://acme.example.com/license",
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `license_id` (String) The SPDX-style license ID (the natural key, e.g. `Acme-1.0`). Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `name` (String) The name of the license. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

### Optional

- `comment` (String) A comment about the license. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `fsf_libre` (Boolean) Whether the license is considered libre by the Free Software Foundation. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `header` (String) The standard license header. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `osi_approved` (Boolean) Whether the license is approved by the Open Source Initiative. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `see_also` (List of String) A list of URLs with more information about the license. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `template` (String) The standard license template. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `text` (String) The full text of the license. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the license (same as `license_id`)
- `uuid` (String) The UUID of the license, assigned by Dependency-Track

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Licenses can be imported using their license ID (the SPDX-style natural key)
terraform import dependencytrack_license.example Acme-1.0
```
